info.description: Sprint-02 identity, tenant, and audit-read service.
info.title: Auth Service

schema.title: Response List Users Admin Users Get

get.summary: List users in the caller's tenant (RLS-scoped, paginated)

post.summary: Invite a new user; creates the Keycloak user and the DB row

get.summary: Read one user in the caller's tenant

schema.title: Response Deactivate User Admin Users  Sub  Deactivate Post

post.summary: Soft-deactivate a user and revoke their sessions

schema.title: Response Reactivate User Admin Users  Sub  Reactivate Post

put.summary: Set a user's realm roles (tenant_admin only)

get.description:
Paginated, filtered read of audit.events for the caller's tenant.

Pagination cursor is the last seq seen. Pass it as ``cursor`` on the
next call to fetch events with ``seq > cursor``. Combined with
``limit`` this gives stable forward-only paging without skipped rows.

schema.title: Response List Events Audit Events Get

get.summary: List audit events for the caller's tenant

get.description:
Walk the chain, recompute every hash, and report the first divergence
(if any). Returns ``ok``, ``events_checked``, ``last_seq``/``last_hash``,
and on failure ``first_divergence_seq`` + ``divergence_reason`` +
``expected_hash``/``actual_hash``.

schema.title: Response Verify Chain Audit Verify Get

get.summary: Verify the audit chain for the caller's tenant

post.summary: Exchange username + password for an access token

post.description:
Revoke the refresh token, clear the cookie.

If an ``Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`` header is also sent, the
verified ``tid``/``sub`` are used to emit an ``auth.logout`` audit event.
Refresh tokens deliberately don't carry the ``tid`` claim (Keycloak
default), so without the access token we have no verified tenant
context and skip the audit.
post.summary: Revoke refresh token and clear cookie

get.summary: Return verified claims + DB user state

post.summary: Rotate refresh cookie; return a new access token
Onboard a new clinic/tenant; the caller becomes its owner

get.summary: The caller's active tenant (from the JWT tid)

patch.summary: Update tenant profile / branding / contact fields

get.summary: Fetch the tenant logo bytes (for branding / PDF preview)

put.summary: Upload or replace the tenant logo (multipart image, ≤2MB)

post.summary: Link a principal to the tenant with a management role

schema.title: Response Remove Member Tenants  Tenant Id  Members  Sub  Delete

delete.summary: Remove a member from the tenant

post.description:
Validate that the caller may act as ``tenant_id`` and record the switch.

NOTE: the access token's ``tid`` — which drives RLS — is issued by
Keycloak and is single-tenant in the pilot. A full switch therefore
requires the SPA to obtain a token re-scoped to ``tenant_id`` (future
Keycloak per-tenant identity work). This endpoint is the authorization
gate + audit hook for that flow, and returns the selected tenant so the
SPA can update its active-tenant UI immediately.
post.summary: Select the caller's active tenant (must be a member)
